I'll help you find the high-urgency PagerDuty incidents that are still in the 'triggered' state. Let me first get the current state of incidents and then filter them.

tool_calls.name: execute_lisp
tool_calls.input:
{"code":";; First, let's see all PagerDuty incidents to understand the data structure
(def incidents (pagerduty_incidents))
(def high-triggered (filter (fn [i] (and (= (:urgency i) "high") (= (:status i) "triggered"))) incidents))
{:count (count high-triggered)
 :ids (map :id high-triggered)}"}
